authoritySource: OET Speaking intelligibility descriptors v2023 + Dr. Ahmed Hesham pronunciation guide

intelligibility.6: Pronunciation is easily understood; prosodic features (stress, intonation, rhythm) are used effectively with no strain on the listener.
intelligibility.5: Easily understood; a few pronunciation or prosodic errors do not impede communication.
intelligibility.4: Generally understood; pronunciation or prosodic errors and/or L1 accent cause occasional strain.
intelligibility.3: Errors in pronunciation/stress/intonation and/or strong L1 accent cause serious strain for the listener.
intelligibility.2: Often unintelligible. Frequent errors in pronunciation/stress/intonation.

scoreToScaled.note: Overall pronunciation score is a 0-100 composite that maps into the OET Speaking 0-500 scale via lib/scoring.ts:pronunciationProjectedBand().

dimensions.accuracy: Phoneme-level articulation accuracy: how closely each sound matches the reference IPA.
dimensions.fluency: Speech rate and smoothness; absence of unnatural pauses, false starts, or disfluencies.
dimensions.completeness: Proportion of the reference text actually uttered (missed or dropped syllables lower this score).
dimensions.prosody: Word stress, sentence stress, intonation contour, and rhythm. Critical for medical terminology and patient reassurance.
